content: Tell us how you would like to change crafting mechanics ingame.

Recalibration also needs a big overhaul lets be honest. In div 2 you have the library, that would be good here.

Imagine you want a Gold T2 Fury Chest with CHD and Socialite, and after weeks of grinding the ONE weekly mission that drops T2, you find one. It’s the perfect blueprint; you just need to roll a decent, above-average CHC stat into the final dead slot. (we all know how rare this situation is on its own!)

**The math is explicitly rigged to brick that item.**

The Pool: A flat 5.56% chance to hit CHC out of 18 stats.

The Quality: A 50% coin-flip to land in the upper half of that 2.40\% - 6.00\% bracket.

Net Success Rate: 2.78% per pull (a 1-in-36 chance).

Because material costs escalate, an item is effectively "soft-bricked" after a certain number of attempts (depending on your level of whaleage and how many recal mats you've gathered). Using a standard geometric distribution, your odds of failing to get a mediocre roll before the item bricks are staggering:

Within 5 rolls: 86.9% Failure Rate

Within 10 rolls: 75.5% Failure Rate

Within 20 rolls: 57.0% Failure Rate

The pool features zero bad-luck mitigation. Because it rolls with replacement, you can hit the exact same unwanted trash stats back-to-back. Your 20th pull faces the exact same abysmal 2.78% baseline odds as your first, except it now costs a fortune.

When lowering your standards to a completely average, non-maxed stat still results in a 57% chance to permanently ruin a rare drop, the progression loop is fundamentally broken.

Gating both stat selection and value optimization behind a single, exponentially expensive slot machine doesn't create an endgame grind, it just converts rare player wins into frustrating resource sinks.
Its not implemented? We need someone with max lv on an item to check even

referenced_message.author: fearlessdave
referenced_message.content: Crafting is a huge letdown unless you are in a clan with maxed out workbenches. As someone who has been slowly grinding up my own clan levels I can say that it is a huge restriction and forces you to join a clan or just never make progress on your crafting levels. This is compounded even more because you tied the agent career ladder to maxing gear pieces crafting levels. I was stuck at phase 6 for a very long time as a result. 24 hour crafting as the baseline just does not make sense, the limiting factor should be crafting material, not time.

content:
Crafting needs an overhaul, Looking at this from a game dev perspective, i understand money needs to be made. Here are my thoughts.

- If the timer stays, the rewards need to be amped up a ton, not 5% or 10% like by alot, waiting 11Hours - 24 hours should give you something "build defining" not a check list item. Here are my ideas for this.

- OS Crafting - For this example we are going to call the item required to craft these "Broken SHD Watches" this is the item you will use to craft your OS's. Make it expensive as hell, im talking you have to farm legandary, incursions things like that in order to craft it, maybe add tiered blue watches, purple, even yellow. Make it random crafted ones, rather than targeted.

- For weapons and gear, if the timer is staying as a way to fast track this, take a page from warframes book.

Im a strong advocate for just bringing back divison 2s crafting but thats in an ideal world.

Crafting is a huge letdown unless you are in a clan with maxed out workbenches. As someone who has been slowly grinding up my own clan levels I can say that it is a huge restriction and forces you to join a clan or just never make progress on your crafting levels. This is compounded even more because you tied the agent career ladder to maxing gear pieces crafting levels. I was stuck at phase 6 for a very long time as a result. 24 hour crafting as the baseline just does not make sense, the limiting factor should be crafting material, not time.

T2 items should be craftable, but mats needed to craft them should exclusively be obtained by dismantling T2 items. 

This makes crafting worth something by making it possible to get useable gear at the highest level.

It also means players MUST farm multiple T2 items before they can craft one.

content: Give us an option to have a targeted stats option for those who pays for warden. This way we have a reason to keep paying for it monthly. As of now it's pretty useless.

There are alot of changes they should make to help this. I agree with you on that. The core structure can't be mangled. If they fix the recalibrate system the rare T2 items will become easier to get perfect. 
Unfortunately the issues are not a change one thing and all is fixed type of thing.  If the crafting system gets changed the recalibrate system has to be changed to synergize correctly. If they only change crafting, but keep the recalibrate sysyem the same, T2s become harder to get right. There might be alot more people using perfect T0 or T1 weapons and just upgrading them instead of rerolling.

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
Crafting is currently one of the most frustrating systems in the game, primarily because of the combination of long timers and excessive RNG.
First, the crafting times need to be reconsidered. Waiting up to 24 hours for a single crafted item is excessive. Even with a fully upgraded clan, the reduction only cuts that time in half. Players who purchase Warden Supplies can reduce clan crafting to roughly 1 hour and 26 minutes, which feels much more reasonable. In my opinion, no crafted item should take longer than 1 hour and 30 minutes when a player has purchased the crafting-related bonuses. The current timers feel more restrictive than rewarding.
Second, the crafting system needs more player control.
Currently, players can spend large amounts of materials crafting weapons repeatedly and still never receive the combination of attributes and talents they are looking for. This creates a situation where progression is determined almost entirely by luck.
A possible solution would be allowing players to lock specific attributes or talents before crafting by spending additional materials.
For example:
Base weapon craft cost: 1 Superior, 5 Standard, and 20 Worn materials.
Locking 1 attribute: Additional 1 Superior, 2 Standard, and 5 Worn materials.
Locking 1 talent: Additional 1 Superior, 2 Standard, and 5 Worn materials.
If I wanted to craft an AUG A3 Para XS and guarantee the "Multitasking" talent and "Skill Multishot Chance" attribute, I would pay the increased crafting cost, but those selections would be guaranteed while the remaining rolls would still be randomized.
An additional option would be allowing players to lock their primary stat category (Firepower, Toughness, or Engineering) for an additional material cost. This would give players meaningful control over their builds without completely removing the excitement of finding the perfect roll.
I do not believe standard T2 weapons, and gear should become craftable. Endgame players should still have reasons to run Elite activities and chase top-tier rewards. The goal is not to remove progression, but to reduce unnecessary frustration.
The same philosophy should apply to gear crafting. Currently, it is possible to spend dozens of Superior materials and multiple days of crafting time without receiving a usable item. For gear, players could be allowed to lock a gear set and one attribute before crafting. Since gear only has a single talent slot, this would provide targeted progression while still preserving RNG in the remaining stats.
These changes would significantly improve the crafting experience, reduce frustration, and allow players to prepare for endgame content through effort and planning rather than relying almost entirely on luck.

Added: These systems could only be applied for weapons where you have max proficiency. So get a rough Aug level the proficiency to level ten, than after you can custom craft the aug.

content: I gave up on using crafting to try and get gear that's useful. About as effective as running elites. So, not just crafting for finishing the SHD level tasks.
Im 4 weeks in on a good KSG roll. I gave up on trying to calculate the amount of material ive spent.
